mappings.rationaleAI: Megalithic monuments are monumental stone sculptures in the round, serving ceremonial and ritualistic functions, representing primitive sculpture using stone as material.

The Statue of Liberty is a colossal neoclassical sculpture by Bartholdi with Eiffel's iron framework, exemplifying commemorative monumental sculpture and representational human figures in the history of Western sculpture.
Angkor Wat's extraordinary bas-reliefs depicting Hindu epics represent major relief sculpture in stone, with devotional images from non-Western sculptural traditions.
Hatshepsut's mortuary temple at Deir el-Bahri and her monuments, including royal iconographic sculpture and posthumous erasure, exemplify ancient Egyptian commemorative and devotional sculpture.
Cave art includes engravings carved into stone surfaces, representing the earliest sculptural techniques alongside painting, relevant to primitive sculpture history.
Dadaism's readymades (Duchamp), collage, and anti-art sculptural objects challenged conventional sculpture, relevant to the history of nonrepresentational and Western sculpture.
Greek mythology's representation across centuries of Western sculpture -- from devotional images to portraits to decorative works -- makes it relevant to the iconography and history of sculpture.
The Vienna Secession connected decorative sculpture to modernist art and design, with Klimt's Beethoven Frieze and the Secession Building's sculptural elements.
Etruscan distinctive art includes tomb sculptures and figures that influenced Roman sculptural traditions, relevant to the history of Western sculpture.
